content.problemStatement:
A permutation of N **distinct** integers between 1 and N, both inclusive, is called a prime permutation of size N iff - for all i between 1 and N, the following condition holds:

The ith integer is the Xth smallest integer in the first i integers, where X is either 1 or a prime number.

Your task is to find out how many prime permutations are there of size N.
constraints: Each input file will not be larger than 4 MB (4,000,000,000 bytes) in size.
**WARNING!** Large I/O files. Use fast I/O methods.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, denoting the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of prime permutations of size N. Since the answers can be very large, output each answer modulo 1,000,000,007.
